title: ListChanges
description: Delivery mechanisms need a way to show the current working set at a glance, filtering out shelved and discarded items automatically. The `ListChanges` use case retrieves all active changes — those that have not been drafted or discarded — from the default workspace, sorted by creation order.
context: Delivery mechanisms need a way to show the current working set at a glance, filtering out shelved and discarded items automatically. The `ListChanges` use case retrieves all active changes — those that have not been drafted or discarded — from the default workspace, sorted by creation order.

rules: `ListChanges.execute()` MUST return all changes that are neither drafted nor discarded. The result MUST be sorted by creation order, oldest first.
The returned `Change[]` MUST contain artifact state (status, validated hashes) but MUST NOT include artifact file content. Content is loaded on demand through separate use cases.
`ListChanges` MUST accept a `ChangeRepository` as its sole constructor argument. It MUST delegate to `ChangeRepository.list()` to retrieve changes.

rules.requirement: Returns an empty array when no active changes exist
rules: When the repository contains no active changes (all are drafted, discarded, or none exist), `execute()` MUST return an empty array. It MUST NOT throw.

scenarios.name: Constructor accepts ChangeRepository
then: it requires a ChangeRepository in its constructor

scenarios.name: Multiple active changes exist
given: the repository contains three active changes created in order: alpha, beta, gamma
then: the result contains all three changes in creation order: alpha, beta, gamma

scenarios.name: Drafted and discarded changes are excluded
given: the repository contains active change alpha, drafted change beta, and discarded change gamma

scenarios.name: Changes have artifact state but no content
when: execute() returns a list of changes
then: each Change has its artifact map populated with status and validated hashes
AND no artifact file content is loaded

scenarios.requirement: Returns an empty array when no active changes exist
scenarios.name: Repository is empty
given: the repository contains no changes at all
then: the result is an empty array

scenarios.requirement: Returns an empty array when no active changes exist
scenarios.name: All changes are drafted or discarded
given: the repository contains only drafted and discarded changes
then: the result is an empty array

optimizedDescription: Delivery mechanisms need a way to show the current working set at a glance, filtering out shelved and discarded items automatically.
optimizedContext:
# ListChanges

## Rules
- Returns all active changes: ListChanges.execute() MUST return all changes that are neither drafted nor discarded.
- Returns Change entities without content: The returned Change[] MUST contain artifact state (status, validated hashes) but MUST NOT include artifact file content.
- Constructor accepts a ChangeRepository: ListChanges MUST accept a ChangeRepository as its sole constructor argument.
- Returns an empty array when no active changes exist: When the repository contains no active changes (all are drafted, discarded, or none exist), execute() MUST return an empty array.
- Dependencies: core:change, core:kernel

## Constraints
- None explicit.
